questionHtml:
It can be assumed that the survey results are representative of the population of the city.

Ten people in the city are chosen at random. Find the probability that at least five of them prefer dark chocolate.
markschemeHtml:
P(dark chocolate)=88160 (=0.55) (maybe seen in part (d))          (A1)

recognize that the variable has a Binomial distribution          (M1)

X~B(10,0.55)

recognition that P(X≥5) or 1-P(X≤4) is required          (M1)

 

Note: These two M marks are independent of each other.

 

0.738437…

0.738          A1

 

[4 marks]

meta.title: Ten people in the city are chosen at random. Find the probability that at least five of them prefer dark chocolate.
